metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add an AC for `CLAUDE.local.md` (stating what worktree guidance it must contain) or explicitly remove it from Scope and note it is handled by the CLAUDE.md AC.
findings.finding: `CLAUDE.local.md` appears in the Scope section as a file to edit but has no corresponding Acceptance Criterion. An executing agent must decide independently whether to verify this file and what 'done' looks like for it.
findings.location: Scope section ('CLAUDE.local.md (local-only; not committed) — worktree guidance'); Acceptance Criteria (no AC for CLAUDE.local.md)
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '`CLAUDE.local.md` worktree guidance instructs the agent to `cd` into the created worktree and run all subsequent commands from inside it (mirrors CLAUDE.md AC).' Or drop it from Scope with a note: 'CLAUDE.local.md is user-managed and outside repo scope; CLAUDE.md AC covers the equivalent committed guidance.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC item for `CLAUDE.local.md` that specifies what the worktree/`cd` guidance must say (even if it just mirrors the CLAUDE.md AC), or explicitly remove it from Scope with a rationale (e.g. 'local-only files are out of scope for verifiable ACs').
findings.finding: CLAUDE.local.md is listed in the plan's Scope section as a target but has no corresponding Acceptance Criterion. Since it is gitignored and local-only, there is no verifiable AC a reviewer can check, and the edit target is underspecified (no description of what the worktree/cd guidance should say there vs. CLAUDE.md).
findings.location: Scope section: 'CLAUDE.local.md (local-only; not committed) — worktree guidance'; Acceptance Criteria section (no AC item for CLAUDE.local.md)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] `CLAUDE.local.md` (local-only) instructs the agent to `cd` into the created worktree and run all subsequent commands from inside it, consistent with the `CLAUDE.md` instruction.' Alternatively, move CLAUDE.local.md out of Scope with the note that it cannot be verified in CI/plan review.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an Acceptance Criterion for `CLAUDE.local.md` stating that its worktree guidance instructs the agent to `cd` into the created worktree and run all subsequent commands from inside it (mirroring the CLAUDE.md AC).
findings.finding: CLAUDE.local.md is listed in Scope as a target file for worktree guidance changes, but no Acceptance Criterion covers it — the implementer has no verifiable gate to confirm this file was updated, so it can be silently dropped without failing any AC.
findings.location: Scope section (CLAUDE.local.md bullet) and Acceptance Criteria section (no corresponding AC item)
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] `CLAUDE.local.md` worktree guidance instructs the agent to `cd` into the created worktree and run all subsequent commands from inside it.' as an AC item immediately after the CLAUDE.md AC items.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete grep-based proving command for each content AC (e.g., `grep -q 'committed HEAD' docs/repo-snapshot-gates.md`) so every criterion has a machine-readable pass/fail signal.
findings.finding: ACs 1–7 describe content that must appear in specific files but supply no concrete proving command (e.g., a `grep` invocation) to produce machine-readable evidence of success. An agent must eyeball each file with no reproducible signal.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a 'Verification' section listing one grep per content AC, e.g.:
```
grep -q 'committed HEAD' docs/repo-snapshot-gates.md
grep -q 'cd into' docs/repo-snapshot-gates.md
grep -q 'committed HEAD' docs/llm-framework.md
grep -q 'cd into' CLAUDE.md
grep -q 'cd ' examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md
grep -q 'cd ' examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md
make lint
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either update ADR-0005's 'cwd/branch decoupling' note to be consistent with the ref=HEAD-relative-to-invoking-checkout clarification, or add an explicit forward reference in ADR-0005 pointing to docs/repo-snapshot-gates.md as the authoritative source for close-gate checkout semantics.
findings.finding: ADR-0005's 'cwd/branch decoupling' note is explicitly scoped as optional/out-of-scope, leaving it as a potentially contradictory stale reference after docs/repo-snapshot-gates.md is updated to clarify that HEAD is resolved relative to the invoking checkout.
findings.location: Scope section: 'reconciling ADR-0005's decoupling note is optional'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line note in ADR-0005 near the decoupling statement, e.g.: 'Note: for the CLI close path, HEAD is resolved relative to the invoking checkout — see docs/repo-snapshot-gates.md for details.' This keeps the ADR accurate without requiring a full reconciliation pass.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
